summary: Matched and linked the game-mode registration common translation unit, gmregcommon.c. The PR switched melee/gm/gmregcommon.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py, added the missing data definition for lbl_803D79F0 in the C source plus an extern declaration in the header, and corrected several string symbols in symbols.txt to local @-style labels needed for the linked match.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so Object(NonMatching, "melee/gm/gmregcommon.c") became Object(Matching, "melee/gm/gmregcommon.c"), linking the TU as matched.
Added a global u8 data array named lbl_803D79F0 in src/melee/gm/gmregcommon.c with 24 byte values: 0 through 17, then 20, 21, 22, 24, 25, and 33.
Added extern u8 lbl_803D79F0[]; to src/melee/gm/gmregcommon.h so the newly defined data can be referenced across translation units.
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt to mark several string/data labels as local symbols: lbl_803D7A08 to @75, lbl_803D7A40 to @76, and lbl_804D4050 to @77.
Preserved nearby local jump table symbols jumptable_803D7A50 and jumptable_803D7A70 while only changing the labels needed by this match.
smart_moves: The PR did the minimal set of linkage changes needed for a match: promote the object in configure.py, define the missing data in the C source, expose it through the header, and adjust symbol-map locality.
Using an actual C definition for lbl_803D79F0 rather than leaving it only as a symbol-map object made gmregcommon.c self-contained enough to link as Matching.
Changing string labels in symbols.txt from global lbl_* names to local @75/@76/@77 labels aligns with how compiler-emitted local constants are represented in matched code.
The added array uses u8 and explicit byte values, matching the existing symbol-map evidence that lbl_803D79F0 is a .data byte object of size 0x18.
decomp_lessons: Before switching a TU from NonMatching to Matching, verify that any referenced .data/.sdata objects are actually defined in C or otherwise correctly represented for the linker.
Symbol-map cleanup can be part of matching: compiler-local constants should often become local @N symbols with scope:local rather than remain generic global lbl_* entries.

When promoting a matched source file, update both the build configuration and any header declarations needed by other files; otherwise linkage may succeed locally but fail when other TUs reference the data.
The symbol size can guide data reconstruction: lbl_803D79F0 had size 0x18, which corresponds to the 24-byte u8 initializer added in gmregcommon.c.
naming_conventions: Unidentified global data retained the decomp placeholder name lbl_803D79F0.
Local compiler-generated constants in symbols.txt were represented with @N names such as @75, @76, and @77 and annotated with scope:local.
Existing gm function naming remained unchanged, including gm_8017BE84, gm_8017BE8C, and gmRegSetupEnemyColorTable.
The header declaration used the same placeholder symbol name as the source definition: extern u8 lbl_803D79F0[].
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Promote the object from NonMatching to Matching only after the required data layout and symbols are represented in source and symbols.txt.
Use the symbol map's object type and size metadata to choose the source type and initializer length; here, a .data byte object of size 0x18 became a 24-element u8 array.
Convert global-looking string labels to local @ labels when matching indicates they are function-local or compiler-local constants.
Keep local jump tables marked scope:local in symbols.txt while adjusting adjacent string constants, avoiding unnecessary churn.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the provided PR slice.
The PR had one review and was merged quickly, but the dump does not provide reviewer text or rationale.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or type lbl_803D79F0 in gmregcommon.
Search for other PRs that switch game-mode files from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py.
Check whether @75, @76, or @77 are later given semantic names or remain local symbols.
Look for follow-up work on gmregclear.c, gm_1832.c, or gm_18A5.c, which remained NonMatching near gmregcommon in configure.py.
